text: ⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 🇺🇸 Nidaa Al-Watan: The trilateral meeting of Israeli, Lebanese and U.S. ambassadors in Washington represents a decisive stage in setting the framework for direct negotiations. Lebanon insists on a ceasefire, even temporary, before any talks, calling it essential for a balanced process. Israel, however, pushes for negotiations focused on Hezbollah’s disarmament while maintaining military pressure – effectively negotiating under fire. A Lebanese presidential source rejected Hezbollah leader Naim Qassem’s call to cancel the meeting, stating that Lebanon is trying to stop the bloodshed and repair the mistake of others (Hezbollah) who launched the war. Lebanon is not making concessions but defending its sovereignty. A Lebanese government source said the U.S. has committed to pressuring Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to agree to a ceasefire in southern Lebanon. The meeting’s success hinges on securing such a ceasefire, which would then allow broader talks. @wfwitness

🇷🇺 An SBU agent who was transmitting data about the objects of the Black Sea Fleet has been detained in Crimea The FSB also detained three participants in the preparation of a terrorist attack against a high-ranking security official in Moscow 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

🇨🇳 🇮🇷 ⚡️ 🇺🇸 A Chinese tanker sanctioned by the United States passed through the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday despite a U.S. blockade on the chokepoint, shipping data showed. The Rich Starry would be the first to make it through the strait and to exit the Persian Gulf since the blockade began, data from LSEG, Marine Traffic and Kpler showed.

North Korea test-fired five missiles from its new 5,000-ton Choe Hyon-class destroyer off the west coast. The launch included 2 strategic cruise missile and 3 anti-ship missiles. State media said all missiles hit their targets with “ultra-precision.”

↩️ Reply to OSINT Michael A. Horowitz RT @glcarlstrom : A temporary moratorium on enrichment is probably a workable compromise. You just have to haggle over the duration: Iran won't accept 20 years; Trump can't sell five years. But you have a bunch of other issues beyond that: - the HEU is of course the big one. Iran wants to downblend rather than ship it out, which doesn't seem like a dealbreaker (it loses the stockpile either way). But who's going to monitor that process, on what timetable will it occur, etc. - then you have other issues around enrichment and weaponization: can Iran continue to develop centrifuges? What about other aspects of R&D? Does it have to dismantle any nuclear facilities or refrain from building new ones? Again, how is all this monitored? Also notable that there's no mention at all of missiles and proxies. These were supposedly red lines for America in the February talks. But they don't feature at all in any of the reporting (including mine) about the current negotiations. That's probably realistic. Iran won't negotiate around missiles, and even if Iran agreed to concessions around proxies, they were always going to be hard to verify. Still, notable that they've disappeared. https://www.nytimes.com/2026/04/13/us/politics/us-iran-deal.html tweet

The U.S. and Indonesia launched a new “Major Defense Cooperation Partnership” (MDCP) to deepen military ties in the Indo-Pacific. It focuses on: Military modernization (tech, maritime systems, MRO support). Training & education (including special forces programs). Expanded joint exercises & interoperability. Separately, the two countries are in early discussions about possible U.S. military overflight access in Indonesian airspace.

A U.S.-sanctioned Chinese tanker passed through the Strait of Hormuz despite the U.S. blockade, according to shipping data. The vessel, Rich Starry, carrying about 250,000 barrels of methanol, is the first known tanker to exit the Gulf since the blockade began.

The defense pact between Pakistan and Saudi Arabia appears uneven. Pakistan is explicitly obligated to send military forces to defend Saudi Arabia if requested, with clear language outlining that responsibility. By contrast, there is no equally clear clause requiring Saudi Arabia to defend Pakistan in return. Source: Drop Site News

OSINTdefender U.S. President JD Vance, who considers himself a devout Catholic, was asked earlier about the administration’s recent public conflict with The Vatican and Pope Leo XIV, as well as last night’s picture posted by President Trump depicting himself as Jesus Christ. Vance responded that he thinks the picture was a “joke” by the President, and that The Vatican should stick to matters of “morality and the Catholic Church” and not the policy of the United States. tweet

Senator Graham on Iran: To the Pope: you’re a good, holy man. You don’t understand the evil of Iran—you’re miscalculating here. The Catholic Church—God bless it—in the 1930s didn’t really get Hitler. And to the Pope: you really don’t get this regime—the Ayatollah and his henchmen.

Senator Graham on Iran: Their reason for living is to die. They want to die a martyr’s death. They want to bring back the missing Mahdi—the 12th Imam—who will come back and save the world, in their view, through chaos.

Visioner RT @NSTRIKE1231 : 📉 🛢️ Oil production in Russia is stagnating, linked to Ukrainian airstrikes and uncertainty in U.S. policy. ▪️ In March, Russian producers pumped less than 9.2 million barrels per day on average — roughly the same as in February and about 0.5 million barrels below the level allowed under international quotas. ▪️ Kyiv has reportedly managed to disable at least 40% of the infrastructure capacity used for crude oil exports. ▪️ The Bell also points to the impact of unclear U.S. policy. In March, the Trump administration temporarily allowed purchases of Russian oil, but the measure expired on April 11, and Washington has not yet announced whether it will be extended. See the latest updates with us: @NSTRIKE1231 tweet

🇷🇺 Russian Rostec has tested the technology of group use of strike drones. Rostec specialists have developed a technology for the group use of strike drones. The devices, created on the basis of Supercam drones, can now automatically exchange information about targets. The data transmission systems on the drones have been modified for this purpose. The new complex has already undergone preliminary tests at the test site. The devices successfully hit a number of targets. "During the tests, a group of drones patrolled the area in search mode. The first drone to detect a target transmitted the data to the rest of the drones in the complex. Then the target was confirmed by a human operator, after which it was hit by all the drones in the group. As part of this technology, one person can control up to 10 patrolling munitions at once. When a target is detected, the system's neural network itself 'identifies' the targets and distributes tasks to the patrolling munitions - in what order they attack and which of the drones conducts objective control. The system tests will continue" In the future, the technology of group interaction of drones may be effective for overcoming the enemy's air defense system, as well as for guaranteed destruction of the most complex targets with a concentrated strike. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics
